text: The robotic excision of a gastrointestinal stromal tumor is underway in a 63-year-old male. The sigmoid colon is retracted, and the peritoneum to the right of the colorectal junction is scored using monopolar electrocautery. The right ureter is identified with endocyanine green injection. The surgeon carefully spares the hypogastric nerves while developing the holy plane posterior to the mesorectum and anterior to the presacral fascia.
The surgeon begins anterior dissection by scoring the peritoneum between the urinary bladder and the rectum. As the dissection deepens, the gastrointestinal stromal tumor is visually identified.

The assistant performs a digital rectal exam to verify the depth of dissection within the pelvis. The tumor is carefully freed from its adhesion to the rectum and prostate, ensuring the capsule remains intact. Sharp dissection is used to completely mobilize the tumor off the pelvic floor.
The actual size of the tumor is much smaller than what appears on the robotic screen.
